scope_role: cross-process overlay linking legal/procedural and research-integrity contamination
representative_titles: AI now drafts completely absurd complaints; police lawyer frustrated: cases are cited that; Journal taking corrective actions after learning author used ChatGPT to update references; Medical journal publishes a letter on AI with a fake reference to itself
paper_thesis_use: Supports a reviewer-visible note that fabricated or unverifiable references appear across both legal/procedural and scholarly workflows without yet justifying a new top-level category.
dashboard_hook: Table 1E / research-integrity bridge note; process-stage heatmap across filing, references, correction, and retraction.
why_not_top_level_label: The pattern is real locally but still rides mostly on one broadcaster route plus Springer/Retraction Watch linked notices; it is better modeled as an overlay than a new top-level class.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Capture one non-Springer or direct publisher/institution notice outside the current cluster, then decide whether to patch the shared taxonomy live bundle from zero-seed to staged-shadow status.
